# Community from chaos

# Overview

For most of TWOLD’s design, I’ve gravitated toward a baseline of community, yet I have always struggled to flesh out that community.

On the other hand, I’ve always enjoyed stories where family and community form from unexpected circumstances.

# Aliens

One of my favorite examples of this is *Aliens*.

In the midst of a nightmarish war for survival, a family unit subtly emerges.

<aside>
💡

This is a child of [Society vs Adventure](../Society%20vs%20Adventure%2022758e628ba280849aefd9a002bf488f.md).

</aside>

What if I took a step back, went all out with a baseline of chaos, and then built order and community on top of that?

# Order and chaos

One of the lessons I have learned from life (and particularly over a decade of procedural world generation) is it’s practically impossible to house train chaos.

<aside>
💡

It is easier to turn chaos into order than it is to turn order into chaos.

</aside>

The only practical direction is to add a splash of chaos to order.

However, in this particular storytelling context, I don’t mean true chaos and order, but *stylized* chaos and order.

The chaos will only appear chaotic.  It will actually be designed and implemented in an orderly manner.

# The Agency

While [The Agency](../../../Features/The%20Agency%2044db7ec455e14b10a9bc1da088f20f66.md) involves a degree of chaos, it’s existence also assumes a degree of order.

## Balk

I had a character I created in my Teens named Balk.  He kind of looked like a duck.

His main gimmick was he partially existed in a parallel reality where everything was just business, and treated all of the bizarre situations he was in as though it was routine business.

The trick was, the rules of logic bent around him and obeyed the business-theme.

<aside>
💡

This made Balk effectively immune to the dangers of adventure.

Adventure would be happening all around Balk and he would be methodically soldiering through it as though it were another day at the office.

</aside>

<aside>
💡

Fugue is mildly a spiritual successor to Balk.  He doesn’t lay heavily into the business mechanic but still partially exists in his own reality.

</aside>

I’m wondering if I can do a similar thing here with the Agency?

# Injected stability

I think I may just be reinventing [Injected stability](../../../Features/Injected%20stability%2054190f7026bf4beb96bc5ef39de248ee.md).

Except the idea isn’t fully invented yet, which is probably I’m still stumbling around trying to figure out the boundaries.

This also relates to [Chaotic world](../../../Features/Chaotic%20world%2015258e628ba2805abd70e0c63f03c571.md).
